concentration.headline: Strong urban primacy — capital drives the country
concentration.body: Buenos Aires dominates Argentina's urban hierarchy with 34.9% of tracked urban population across 502 cities. The gap between Buenos Aires and the second city is substantial, reflecting a historically centralised national structure.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: France, Mexico, South Korea

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Buenos Aires & Lanús
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Buenos Aires and Lanús (11 km apart, combined population 17,169,263). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 11 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Argentina shows clear urban primacy: Buenos Aires accounts for 34.9% of tracked population. The gravity network (Buenos Aires → Lanús, avg 11 km) reinforces this — economic mass concentrates in and around the dominant city, with secondary cities playing supporting rather than competing roles.
